text.title: token-organization Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for token-organization. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Organize all tokens into exactly three tiers; never reference a primitive token directly from a component.
rule.rationale: Skipping the semantic layer collapses theming into a global search-and-replace; the semantic tier is the only place where "meaning" lives and can be swapped per theme.

rule.statement: Name tokens using the pattern `{category}.{property}.{variant}.{state}` with all segments in kebab-case.
rule.rationale: Self-documenting names eliminate the need for external lookup; `color.surface.primary.hover` conveys category, property, variant, and state without a legend.

rule.statement: Start with the minimum token set required to implement the design system; add tokens only when a genuine gap exists, not speculatively.
rule.rationale: Token bloat reduces discoverability and defeats the systematization purpose; every token without a consumer is maintenance debt.

rule.statement: Require at least one designer and one engineer to approve any new token before it enters the source-of-truth file.
rule.rationale: Token conventions collapse into forks when individual contributors add tokens unilaterally; dual approval enforces naming and tier discipline without a bottleneck.

rule.statement: Provide a deprecation alias for any renamed token for at least one release cycle before removal.
rule.rationale: Instant removal of renamed tokens breaks builds silently across every consuming codebase; deprecation aliases give teams time to migrate.

rule.statement: Before any global token rename, produce a migration plan with codemods — do not do a bulk rename mid-flight when engineers are blocked.
rule.rationale: Mass rename without codemod support destroys team velocity and erodes trust in the design system.

rule.statement: When auditing an existing token set, output a diff plan (rename map + deprecation aliases) for human approval before making any bulk writes.
rule.rationale: Automated bulk renames without human review risk breaking all consuming codebases; the diff plan separates analysis from execution.

rule.statement: When classifying tokens into tiers, flag tokens that share the same value but appear in different tiers as candidates for deduplication.
rule.rationale: Duplicate values across tiers indicate missing aliases; the tier system only works when primitives are referenced by aliases, not duplicated.
